DefaultCmPageTemplate

Composes GlobalHeader + SideNavigation + GlobalFooter into the base page shell every Figma Make prototype is built inside. The header scrolls away with the page; SideNavigation is position: sticky and tracks how much of the header is still visible so its bottom edge (and pin button) always stays flush with the viewport; the footer lands at the bottom on short pages via the standard "sticky footer" flex trick.

Figma node 17:5086 import { DefaultCmPageTemplate } from "figma-make-bridge" Source ↗

Live preview

Rendered inside its own <iframe> (its own real viewport and scroll container), fixed at 1040px wide so it clears the 960px desktop breakpoint and SideNavigation shows — scroll horizontally if your window is narrower. Enough placeholder content is included to scroll vertically too — try it, to see the header scroll away while SideNavigation stays pinned, and hover/click the rail exactly as on its own page.

Content slot

The "Page Content & Paddings" region: max-width 1280px, centered, 16px left/right + 24px top + 32px bottom padding. A decorative 12-column grid overlay in the source Figma frame is a design-time guide only and isn't reproduced here, per that component's own Figma description.

Props

PropTypeDefaultNotes
childrenReactNode—Required. Rendered into the content slot.
headerPropsOmit<GlobalHeaderProps, "className">—
sideNavPropsOmit<SideNavigationProps, "className">—
footerPropsOmit<GlobalFooterProps, "className">—

Below 960px, SideNavigation is hidden entirely (no drawer/overlay state exists in the source Figma file) — the shell always composes all three sub-components, there's no "sidenav-less full-width" variant.
